content.problemStatement:
An array BBB is said to be stingy if no two of its adjacent elements are equal.

That is, BBB is stingy iff Bi≠Bi+1B_i \neq B_{i+1}Bi​=Bi+1​ for each 1≤i<∣B∣1 \leq i \lt |B|1≤i<∣B∣.

Alice and Bob have an array AAA with them, and decided to play a game on it.

They alternate turns, with Alice going first.

On their turn, a player chooses any subsequence SSS of AAA, such that SSS is a **maximum length** stingy subsequence of AAA, and deletes it from AAA.

The remaining elements don't change their order.

This game continues as long as AAA is not empty, with the winner being the player who makes the last move.

With optimal play, who wins?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a two lines of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN, the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​: the elements of array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the winner: either `Alice` or `Bob`.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings `BOB`, `Bob` ,`boB`, and `bob` will all be treated as equivalent.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Alice chooses the subsequence [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] on her turn and deletes it. The remaining array is [1][1][1], which Bob chooses and ends the game.
Test case 222: Alice can choose the entire array on her turn.
Test case 333: The only stingy subsequence is [2][2][2], so both players must repeatedly choose it. Bob makes the last move and wins.
Test case 444: Alice chooses the stingy subsequence [2,4,5,4][2, 4, 5, 4][2,4,5,4] with indices (1,3,4,5)(1, 3, 4, 5)(1,3,4,5) and deletes it. The remaining array is [4,4][4, 4][4,4].
Bob can only choose [4][4][4] now, leaving Alice with the array [4][4][4] which she can clear out and win.
